title: Software Architect
description:
**About Air Apps**
 At Air Apps, we believe in thinking bigger- and moving faster. We’re a family-founded company on a mission to create the world’s first AI-powered Personal & Entrepreneurial Resource Planner (PRP), and we need your passion and ambition to help us change how people plan, work, and live. Born in Lisbon, Portugal, in 2018 - and now with offices in both Lisbon and San Francisco - we’ve remained self-funded while reaching over 100 million downloads worldwide.
   

  

 Our long-term focus drives us to challenge the status quo every day, pushing the boundaries of AI-driven solutions that truly make a difference. Here, you’ll be a creative force, shaping products that empower people across the globe.
   

  

 Join us on this journey to redefine resource management—and change lives along the way.
   

  

* This is a fully onsite position, based at our office in Lisbon, where you will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams in person and contribute to a dynamic and fast-paced environment. We are open to support with relocation efforts.


**The Role**
 As a
 **Software Architect / Solutions Architect** 
 at Air Apps, you will be responsible for
 **defining and overseeing the overall system architecture** 
 for our AI-powered platform. You will collaborate with engineering teams, product managers, and stakeholders to design
 **scalable, secure, and high-performing solutions** 
 that align with business objectives.
   

  

 You will play a key role in shaping our
 **technology strategy, evaluating new tools and frameworks, and ensuring best practices** 
 in software design, cloud infrastructure, and system integration.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Define and maintain the overall architecture and system design for scalable and efficient software solutions.
* Provide technical leadership to engineering teams, guiding them in best practices for architecture, coding, and system scalability.
* Design and document high-level system diagrams, data flows, and API structures.
* Evaluate and recommend technologies, frameworks, and cloud services to optimize performance, security, and cost-efficiency.
* Ensure high availability, fault tolerance, and security in all architectural decisions.
* Oversee system integrations, including microservices, APIs, and third-party services.
* Work closely with DevOps and SRE teams to ensure smooth deployment and operational reliability.
* Establish coding and architecture standards, ensuring maintainability and scalability of codebases.
* Identify and mitigate technical risks, ensuring system resilience and future scalability.
* Conduct architecture reviews and performance audits to continuously improve system efficiency.


**Requirements**
* Around 6+ years of experience in software development and architecture.
* Strong expertise in system design, cloud computing, and microservices architecture.
* Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud Platform) and cloud-native architectures.
* Proficiency in backend technologies (e.g., Node.js, Python, Java, .NET) and experience with modern frontend frameworks (e.g., React, Angular, Vue).
* Knowledge of databases (SQL and NoSQL), caching strategies, and data pipelines.
* Experience with event-driven architectures, API design, and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes).
* Strong understanding of security best practices, identity management, and encryption techniques.
* Ability to evaluate trade-offs in architectural decisions, balancing scalability, cost, and complexity.
* Familiarity with DevOps, CI/CD pipelines, and Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, CloudFormation).
* Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain complex architectural concepts to technical and non-technical stakeholders.


**What benefits do we offer?**
* Apple hardware ecosystem for work.
* Annual Bonus
* Top-tier Health and Life Insurance for peace of mind.
* Transportation Budget to support your commute needs.
* Coverflex benefits package for meal allowances, well-being, and more.
* Childcare support.
* Air Conference - an opportunity to meet the team, collaborate, and grow together.
* Pension Fund to support your long-term financial planning.
* Urban Sports Club membership to keep you active.
* Meals 100% free at the hub.


**Diversity & Inclusion**
 At Air Apps, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace. We enthusiastically welcome applicants from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We celebrate diversity in all its forms and believe that varied voices and experiences make us stronger.
   

  

**Application Disclaimer**
 At Air Apps, we value transparency and integrity in our hiring process. Applicants must submit their own work without any AI-generated assistance. Any use of AI in application materials, assessments, or interviews will result in disqualification.

title: Lead Production Support Engineer| Java | Stockholm, Sweden
description:
**Role** 
 – Lead Production/Application Support Engineer
   

  

**Technology** 
 – Java (Spring batch, JSP, Hibernate) HTML & JavaScript/jQuery, Microservices, Handling 24x7 operations, critical incidents, Monitoring & observability tools (APM, logs, alerts)
   

  

**Location** 
 – Stockholm, Sweden
   

  

**Business Unit** 
 – CMTADM
   

  

**Compensation** 
 – Competitive (including bonus)
   

  

**Job Description**
 Today, the corporate landscape is dynamic and the world ahead is full of possibilities! None of the amazing things we do at Infosys would be possible without an equally amazing culture, the environment where ideas can flourish and where you are empowered to move forward as far as your ideas will take you.
   

  

 At Infosys, we assure that your career will never stand still, we will inspire you to build what’s next and we will navigate further together. Our journey of learnability, values and trusted relationships with our clients continue to be the cornerstones of our organization and these values are upheld only because of our people.
   

  

**Your role**
 In the role of Lead Application Support Engineer with focus on Java-based applications, you will be responsible for proactive identification of issues in production environment leveraging observability tools with the aim of reducing MTTD and MTTR. You will also provide regular support/guidance to project teams on complex coding, issue resolution and execution.
   

  

 You will collaborate with some of the best talent in the industry to create and implement innovative high-quality solutions. You will be part of a learning culture, where teamwork and collaboration are encouraged, excellence is rewarded, and diversity is respected and valued.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Interfacing with technical and business stakeholders – internal and external (Managers / Application owners, Support Analysts, Infrastructure Team, Developers and technology specialists).
* Managing and maintaining production environment.
* Change management and application deployments.
* Restore a normal service operation as quickly as possible and minimize the impact on business operations, thus ensuring that the best possible levels of service quality and availability are maintained.
* Coordinate all activities required to lead incident triage in compliance with SLAs and OLAs.
* Facilitate the outage calls and ensure that all the required resources are engaged to work a Major Incident.
* Preparing internal reports on Major incidents and leading their review with Executive Management.
* SLA & KPI reporting
* Onsite – Offshore coordination.
* Interfacing with technical and business stakeholders – internal and external.
* Configuration, customization and integration of application.
* Client interfacing and offshore coordination.
* Available to be on-call and support production issues.


**Required**
* At least 6+ years of experience with Information Technology.
* Primary skill: Design, develop, test, and maintain Java-based applications.
* Java, Spring boot, Microservices, HTML & JavaScript/jQuery, Groovy, XML,Ubuntu 20., MariaDB 10.3, MSSQL
* Application support and maintenance experience: L2/L3 support, ITIL processes and ITSM tools, Handling 24x7 operations, critical incidents, Monitoring & observability tools, Splunk
* Experience and good understanding of IT SDLC lifecycle phases. Through understanding Agile principles and practices
* Familiar with latest trends in industry from technology and domain standpoint
* Creative, innovative and able to work in a fast-paced environment
* Experience and desire to work in a Global delivery environment.


**Personal**
**Qualifications**
 Besides the professional qualifications, we respect and place equal importance to the candidate’s personality which facilitates success in customer environments. Few traits we look for are:
   

  

* High analytical skills
* A high degree of initiative, flexibility and adaptability
* High customer orientation
* Quality awareness
* Good verbal and written communication skills


**About Infosys**
 Infosys is a global leader in next-generation digital services and consulting. We enable clients in over 50 countries to navigate their digital transformation. With over three decades of experience in managing the systems and workings of global enterprises, we expertly steer our clients through the many next of their digital journey. We do it by enabling the enterprise with an AI-powered core that helps prioritize the execution of change. We also empower the business with agile digital at scale to deliver unprecedented levels of performance and customer delight. Our always-on learning agenda drives their continuous improvement through building and transferring digital skills, expertise and ideas from our innovation ecosystem.
   

  

 Visit www.infosys.com to see how Infosys can help your enterprise navigate your next.
   

  

**All aspects of employment at Infosys are based on merit, competence and performance. We are committed to embracing diversity and creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Infosys is proud to be an equal opportunity employer**

title: Senior Drifttekniker
description:
**Vill du arbeta som senior drifttekniker i komplexa IT-miljöer där stabil drift, felsökning och teknisk förståelse är avgörande?**
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------


Till vår kund, ett väletablerat svenskt IT-tjänsteföretag med lång erfarenhet av drift och outsourcing av verksamhetskritiska IT-miljöer, söker vi nu en senior drifttekniker. Bolaget levererar stabila och säkra IT-tjänster till kunder med höga krav på tillgänglighet och driftssäkerhet.

  

I den här rollen får du arbeta i komplexa IT-miljöer där stabil drift, snabb felsökning och teknisk förståelse är avgörande. Du blir en viktig del av ett tekniskt team som säkerställer att verksamhetskritiska system och applikationer fungerar i produktion.

  

Det här är en roll för dig som trivs nära tekniken, drivs av problemlösning och föredrar en hands-on vardag där din tekniska kompetens gör verklig skillnad.

**Om rollen**
-------------

* Du arbetar med drift, övervakning och felsökning i produktionsmiljöer
* Du identifierar, analyserar och åtgärdar incidenter i både server- och applikationslager
* Du arbetar nära andra tekniker och funktioner för att säkerställa stabil drift och hög tillgänglighet
* Du kombinerar proaktivt stabilitetsarbete med reaktiv incidenthantering
* Du arbetar i komplexa IT-miljöer med höga krav på driftsäkerhet

**Du kommer bland annat att**
-----------------------------

* Drift och övervakning av server- och IT-miljöer i produktion
* Felsökning av system, applikationer och infrastruktur
* Incidenthantering och problemlösning i komplexa miljöer
* Administration av Windows Server och Active Directory
* Virtualiseringsmiljöer (VMware och/eller Hyper-V)
* Nätverksfelsökning (routing, switching, brandväggar)
* Backup, återställning och säkerställande av driftsäkerhet
* Samarbete med andra tekniska team vid applikationsnära incidenter
* Dokumentation av incidenter och lösningar

**Vi tror att du har**
----------------------

* 7–10 års erfarenhet av IT-drift, systemadministration eller liknande roll
* Gedigen erfarenhet av Windows Server och Active Directory
* Vana att arbeta i produktionsmiljöer med höga krav på stabilitet
* Erfarenhet av felsökning i både system- och applikationslager
* Kunskap inom virtualisering (VMware och/eller Hyper-V)
* God förståelse för nätverk och nätverksfelsökning
* Erfarenhet av backup- och lagringslösningar
* Flytande svenska och god engelska i tal och skrift

**Meriterande**
---------------

* PowerShell eller annan scriptning
* Erfarenhet av monitoring- och övervakningsverktyg
* ITIL eller liknande incidentprocesser
* Erfarenhet av hybridmiljöer (on-prem/cloud)
* Erfarenhet från större drift- eller outsourcingmiljöer

**Som person är du**
--------------------

* Prestigelös och trivs i en operativ vardag. Du gillar att arbeta hands-on i tekniska miljöer
* Analytisk och skicklig på felsökning
* Självgående men fungerar bra i team
* Serviceinriktad med fokus på stabil drift och kundnöjdhet

**Övrigt**
----------

* Hybridarbete erbjuds beroende på uppdrag och kundmiljö
* Start: Omgående med hänsyn till uppsägningstid

**Ansökan**
-----------


Vårt Talent Acquisition-team ser gärna att du som söker har goda referenser och ett strukturerat CV där relevant IT-erfarenhet tydligt framgår. Urval och intervjuer sker löpande, så skicka gärna in din ansökan så snart som möjligt.


Har du frågor om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta lovisa@linden-it.se.

title: Junior Project Manager
description:
**Join Polar Point and help shape cybersecurity that makes a real difference**
------------------------------------------------------------------------------


At Polar Point, you will not be one employee among many. You will join a growing cybersecurity company where trust, customer impact and clarity are at the center of everything we do.


We work closely with CISOs, IT leaders and security teams to help organizations strengthen resilience, reduce risk and gain better control of their digital environments. Our work combines strategic advisory, hands-on implementation and long-term customer relationships.


As a Junior Project Manager, you will have the opportunity to support meaningful cybersecurity and IT security assignments, work close to experienced colleagues and contribute to Polar Point’s continued growth. You will help create structure, coordination and progress in customer projects where quality, communication and responsibility matter.

**About Polar Point**
---------------------


Polar Point AB is a Stockholm-based cybersecurity consultancy helping Nordic organizations navigate complexity with clarity. We provide senior expertise, advisory services and practical security solutions that enable customers to manage cyber risk confidently while staying focused on their business.


Our core areas include identity and access management, endpoint security, vulnerability management, security platform implementation, compliance support, security health checks, AI security and project-based cybersecurity delivery.


We are independent, pragmatic and outcome-focused. Our ambition is to be a trusted cybersecurity partner for organizations that need senior guidance, hands-on expertise and clear progress in complex environments.

**About the role**
------------------


As a Junior Project Manager at Polar Point, you will support the planning, coordination and successful delivery of cybersecurity and IT security projects for our customers. You will work closely with senior project managers, consultants, technical experts, vendors and customer stakeholders to help ensure projects are delivered with structure, quality and clear communication.


You will help create progress in assignments where multiple teams, priorities and dependencies need to come together. In practice, this means supporting project plans, follow-up, reporting, meeting coordination, documentation and stakeholder communication.


This role is ideal for someone with a solid project management foundation who enjoys working in a consulting environment, building trusted customer relationships and helping technical and business stakeholders move toward successful outcomes.

**What you will do**
--------------------

### **Project coordination and delivery support**

* Coordinate and support customer projects from initiation through delivery and follow-up.
* Develop and maintain project plans, time plans, status updates, budgets and reporting material.
* Track milestones, deliverables, risks, issues, dependencies and resource needs.
* Facilitate project meetings, workshops, status meetings and follow-up activities with customers and internal teams.
* Ensure clear communication between technical experts, vendors, customers and business stakeholders.
* Prepare project documentation, reports, decision material and executive-level updates when needed.
* Support project governance and help ensure alignment with internal standards and customer requirements.
* Contribute to strong customer relationships by delivering a high level of service, structure and professionalism.

### **Stakeholder coordination and communication**

* Help coordinate stakeholders across customer organizations, Polar Point, partners and vendors.
* Support senior project managers and consultants in planning, prioritization and follow-up.
* Communicate project status, risks and next steps in a clear and structured way.
* Help create clarity in situations where requirements, dependencies or responsibilities are changing.
* Support customer decision-making by preparing concise and relevant information.

### **Practice development and ways of working**

* Contribute to Polar Point’s project methods, templates, routines and delivery practices.
* Help improve how we plan, report and follow up cybersecurity and IT security assignments.
* Share learnings from customer projects and support continuous improvement across the team.
* Work close to experienced colleagues, founders and decision-makers as the company grows.

**Who you are**
---------------


You are a structured and communicative project professional who enjoys creating order, momentum and clarity. You are comfortable coordinating across different teams and stakeholders, and you understand that successful delivery depends on both planning discipline and strong relationships.


You probably enjoy working close to customers, following up details and helping teams move from plans to practical delivery. You take ownership, communicate clearly and care about delivering work that creates value.


You will likely thrive at Polar Point if you:

* want to be part of a growing specialist consultancy rather than a large anonymous organisation
* enjoy creating structure, follow-up and progress in customer assignments
* are comfortable coordinating between technical experts, customers and vendors
* value trust, transparency and long-term relationships
* like working close to experienced colleagues, founders and decision-makers
* want to grow your project management capability in cybersecurity and IT security delivery

**We believe you have**
-----------------------

* Approximately 5 years or more of experience managing or coordinating IT, cybersecurity or technology-related projects.
* Experience working in enterprise environments, consulting environments or complex organizations.
* Demonstrated ability to plan, coordinate and follow up cross-functional projects involving multiple stakeholders, priorities, timelines and risks.
* Good understanding of project management methods such as Agile, Scrum, Waterfall or hybrid delivery models.
* Strong organizational, planning and time management skills.
* Experience using project management tools such as Jira, Microsoft Project, Planner or similar platforms.
* Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively with consultants, technical specialists and customer stakeholders.
* Strong communication and presentation skills in both Swedish and English.
* Fluency in Swedish, written and spoken.
* Ability to obtain and maintain security clearance when required.

**It is a plus if you also have**
---------------------------------

* Previous experience in cybersecurity, information security or IT consulting.
* Experience from large, complex or regulated organizations.
* Experience coordinating projects involving security platforms, identity, endpoint security, vulnerability management, compliance or cloud services.
* Experience preparing reports or decision material for senior stakeholders.
* Experience working with frameworks or regulations such as ISO 27001, NIS2, NIST CSF, DORA or similar.
* Relevant project management certifications such as PMP, PRINCE2, Scrum Master, Agile certification or similar.


Certifications are valuable, but practical experience, sound judgement and proven customer impact matter most to us.

**What we offer**
-----------------


At Polar Point, you will join a senior and entrepreneurial team with high ambitions and low prestige. We offer an environment where you can make a visible impact from day one - both for our customers and for the company we are building.


We offer:

* meaningful cybersecurity and IT security assignments with real customer impact
* close collaboration with experienced consultants, founders and company leadership
* the opportunity to shape our services, methods and ways of working
* a trust-based culture with high responsibility and flexibility
* strong focus on quality, clarity and long-term customer relationships
* room to grow professionally as Polar Point grows
* access to a wider specialist network through our partner ecosystem and alliance relationships
* the chance to help build a cybersecurity consultancy with a clear Nordic identity and strong ambitions

**Location and working model**
------------------------------


The role is based in Stockholm, with flexibility depending on assignment and customer needs. Some work will be performed remotely, while customer presence is expected when it creates value or is required for delivery.


Because many of our customers operate in Swedish-speaking environments, fluent Swedish is required.

**Apply**
---------


If you want to help build a trusted cybersecurity consultancy with senior colleagues, meaningful assignments and real influence, we would like to hear from you.


At Polar Point, you will help customers strengthen security where it matters most - from users and identities to devices, platforms and business-critical environments.

title: Senior Cyber Security Consultant
description:
**Join Polar Point and help shape cybersecurity that makes a real difference**
------------------------------------------------------------------------------


At Polar Point, you will not be one consultant among many. You will join a growing cybersecurity company where senior expertise, trust and customer impact are at the center of everything we do.


We work closely with CISOs, IT leaders and security teams to help organizations strengthen resilience, reduce risk and gain better control of their digital environments. Our work combines strategic advisory, hands-on implementation and long-term customer relationships.


As a Senior Cyber Security Consultant, you will have the opportunity to influence both our customers’ security journeys and Polar Point’s continued growth. You will work in meaningful assignments, contribute to our service offerings and help build a company where technical excellence, clarity and responsibility matter.

**About Polar Point**
---------------------


Polar Point AB is a Stockholm-based cybersecurity consultancy helping Nordic organizations navigate complexity with clarity. We provide senior expertise, advisory services and practical security solutions that enable customers to manage cyber risk confidently while staying focused on their business.


Our core areas include identity and access management, endpoint security, vulnerability management, security platform implementation, compliance support, security health checks, AI security and project-based cybersecurity delivery.


We are independent, pragmatic and outcome-focused. Our ambition is to be a trusted cybersecurity partner for organizations that need senior guidance, hands-on expertise and clear progress in complex environments.

**About the role**
------------------


As a Senior Cyber Security Consultant at Polar Point, you will play a key role in delivering high-value cybersecurity services to our customers while contributing to the growth and development of our consulting practice.


You will combine deep technical understanding with advisory capability and business awareness. In practice, this means that you can move comfortably between strategic discussions with customer leadership, solution design with technical teams and hands-on delivery in complex customer environments.


You will act as a trusted advisor, lead important security initiatives and help customers prioritise improvements that reduce risk and strengthen operational maturity. You will also contribute to business development in a consultative way by identifying customer needs, shaping new engagements and helping develop services that create measurable value.

**What you will do**
--------------------

### **Customer delivery and technical leadership**

* Lead and deliver cybersecurity consulting engagements in complex customer environments.
* Design, implement and improve security solutions across endpoint security, vulnerability management, identity, cloud and hybrid environments.
* Perform security assessments and translate findings into pragmatic, risk-based recommendations.
* Provide senior technical guidance during implementations, security transformations and improvement programmes.
* Help customers strengthen cybersecurity hygiene, visibility, control and operational resilience.
* Support customers in aligning security improvements with business priorities, regulatory expectations and practical implementation constraints.

### **Advisory and project leadership**

* Act as a trusted advisor to CISOs, IT leaders and other senior stakeholders.
* Lead security projects and initiatives from planning and scoping through execution and follow-up.
* Coordinate stakeholders, manage scope, timelines, risks and dependencies.
* Support customer decision-making by explaining complex security topics in a clear and business-relevant way.
* Contribute to structured methods, playbooks, assessments and best practices used across Polar Point engagements.

### **Business development and practice growth**

* Identify customer needs and opportunities within existing and new engagements.
* Support pre-sales activities such as customer meetings, solution design, scoping and effort estimation.
* Contribute to proposals, presentations and service development.
* Help shape and improve Polar Point’s cybersecurity offerings.
* Share knowledge internally and support colleagues as we grow the consulting team.
* Contribute to long-term customer relationships by delivering quality, clarity and trust in every assignment.

**Who you are**
---------------


You are an experienced cybersecurity professional who enjoys combining technical depth with advisory work. You are confident in customer-facing situations and comfortable working with both technical specialists and senior decision-makers.


You probably enjoy solving complex problems, creating structure in uncertain situations and helping customers move from analysis to practical improvement. You take ownership, communicate clearly and care about delivering outcomes that matter.


You will likely thrive at Polar Point if you:

* want to be part of a growing specialist consultancy rather than a large anonymous organisation
* enjoy having influence over both customer delivery and company development
* are comfortable taking senior responsibility in customer engagements
* value trust, transparency and long-term relationships
* like working close to experienced colleagues, founders and decision-makers
* want to combine strategic advisory with hands-on cybersecurity delivery

**We believe you have**
-----------------------

* Approximately 10 years or more of experience in cybersecurity, security consulting or senior security delivery roles.
* Strong experience delivering cybersecurity improvements in customer or enterprise environments.
* Solid knowledge in one or more of the following areas: endpoint security, vulnerability management, identity and access management, cloud security, Microsoft security services, Active Directory, Microsoft Entra ID or security platform implementation.
* Experience leading or contributing to security projects, implementations or improvement programmes.
* Ability to translate technical findings into business-relevant recommendations.
* Experience working with senior stakeholders such as CISOs, CIOs, IT leaders or security managers.
* A consultative mindset and the ability to identify customer needs, shape solutions and contribute to business development.
* Strong communication skills in both Swedish and English.
* Fluency in Swedish, written and spoken.
* Ability to obtain and maintain security clearance when required.

**It is a plus if you also have**
---------------------------------

* Experience from large, complex or regulated organizations.
* Experience with security assessments, security health checks, Zero Trust initiatives or cyber maturity improvement.
* Experience with technologies and platforms such as Microsoft 365 Security, Microsoft Entra ID, Active Directory, Tanium, Trend Vision One, BeyondTrust, Rapid7 or similar tools.
* Experience in IAM, PAM, endpoint management, EDR/XDR, vulnerability management, compliance or security operations.
* Experience working with frameworks or regulations such as ISO 27001, NIS2, NIST CSF, DORA or similar.
* Relevant certifications such as CISSP, CISM, CCSP, Microsoft security certifications or vendor-specific certifications.


Certifications are valuable, but practical experience, sound judgement and proven customer impact matter most to us.

**What we offer**
-----------------


At Polar Point, you will join a senior and entrepreneurial team with high ambitions and low prestige. We offer an environment where you can make a visible impact from day one - both for our customers and for the company we are building.


We offer:

* senior and meaningful cybersecurity assignments with real customer impact
* close collaboration with experienced consultants, founders and company leadership
* the opportunity to shape our services, methods and ways of working
* a trust-based culture with high responsibility and flexibility
* strong focus on quality, clarity and long-term customer relationships
* room to grow professionally as Polar Point grows
* access to a wider specialist network through our partner ecosystem and alliance relationships
* the chance to help build a cybersecurity consultancy with a clear Nordic identity and strong ambitions

**Location and working model**
------------------------------


The role is based in Stockholm, with flexibility depending on assignment and customer needs. Some work will be performed remotely, while customer presence is expected when it creates value or is required for delivery.


Because many of our customers operate in Swedish-speaking environments, fluent Swedish is required.

**Apply**
---------


If you want to help build a trusted cybersecurity consultancy with senior colleagues, meaningful assignments and real influence, we would like to hear from you.


At Polar Point, you will help customers strengthen security where it matters most - from users and identities to devices, platforms and business-critical environments.

title: Junior Platform Engineer
description:
Build something monumental for Healthcare!
   

  

 At Tandem Health we’re reimagining healthcare by putting clinicians first. Our platform - designed by clinicians, for clinicians - is built on deep insight into real-world pain points, with intuitive medical notes and workflows that truly support patient care.
   

  

 We’re a fast-scaling health-tech company backed by top investors and expanding globally. We move fast, stay curious, and believe building something that matters starts with an extraordinary team. If you're passionate about impact and innovation, we'd love to meet you!
   

  

**About The Role**
 You enjoy making systems work, not just writing code, but understanding how everything fits together underneath. You’ve probably spent time tinkering on your own, setting up servers, hosting things yourself, or trying to understand how infrastructure actually behaves in the real world.
   

  

 At Tandem, you’ll join a small, highly skilled platform team and help build the infrastructure that powers the future of European healthcare. You don’t need to know everything from day one - what matters most is your drive to learn, your curiosity, and your willingness to get your hands dirty.
   

  

 This role is focused on infrastructure first. You’ll work closely with experienced engineers, gradually taking ownership of parts of our platform and helping the team move faster by offloading and improving core infrastructure work. Along the way, you’ll learn how to build systems that are reliable, secure, and scalable in a highly regulated environment.
   

  

 If you’re someone who enjoys figuring things out, breaking and fixing systems, and continuously improving how things run - this is a great place to grow.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
* Support the team in building and maintaining cloud infrastructure that powers Tandem’s products.
* Work with Kubernetes-based systems and help improve how services are deployed and operated.
* Contribute to CI/CD pipelines, making deployments smoother and more reliable.
* Assist in implementing infrastructure-as-code using tools like Terraform and Helm.
* Help improve observability, monitoring, and alerting across our systems.
* Take ownership of smaller infrastructure components over time and help scale them.
* Work closely with senior engineers to learn best practices around reliability, security, and performance.


**What You Bring**
* Some experience working with cloud infrastructure (Azure, AWS, or GCP).
* Familiarity with Kubernetes, CI/CD, or infrastructure tooling - even if not at an expert level.
* Exposure to infrastructure-as-code tools like Terraform or Helm.
* Basic programming skills - enough to script, automate, and understand systems.
* A strong curiosity for how systems work under the hood.
* A mindset of learning and improvement - you’re eager to develop your skills quickly.
* Interest in building reliable systems that help others move faster.


**Bonus Points**
* You’ve built or run your own infrastructure projects (e.g. self-hosting, home labs, media servers, etc.)
* Experience working with slightly larger systems or production environments
* Familiarity with networking, security, or observability concepts
* Experience with Kubernetes packaging (e.g. Helm charts)
* Background in software engineering or scripting-heavy roles


 Location
   

  

 We believe the best ideas happen when we’re together. This role is based in our vibrant HQ in the Epicenter building in the heart of Stockholm where you will work primarily from our office to collaborate, connect, and build our culture.
   

  

**How To Apply**
 We adopt a continuous selection process, so please make sure to apply with your CV in English.
   

  

**Our Interview Process Consists Of 4 Stages**
* Screening interview with Talent Acquisition
* Technical interviews with some of our Engineers
* Interview with our CTO & Co-Founder
* Home case and presentation at our office - Join us in the Stockholm office for a lunch, a presentation to experience our our culture firsthand, collaborate with our team, and see how you work in action.


**Benefits**
* Competitive salary & company stock options
* 30 days/year of paid vacation
* 5,000 SEK wellness allowance (friskvårdsbidrag) plus an additional 6,000 SEK yearly to spend on other health related initiatives
* Generous Parental leave top-up for new parents
* Private Medical Insurance to stay healthy
* Mental health support through our partner Mindler
* Pension program
* Social and team-building activities (off-sites, after works, winter/summer parties)
* An opportunity to make a real positive impact in the world of healthcare
* Work with some of the best minds in AI, healthcare, and engineering.


*We review our benefits packages on a regular basis and might modify our benefits from time to time.*
 Culture at Tandem
   

  

 At Tandem, we move fast, think big, and take ownership. We're a high-performing, diverse team with a shared drive to change the future of healthcare - and we’re just getting started.
   

  

 Our culture is built on action, ambition, and learning. You'll be trusted to take the lead, challenge yourself, and make an impact from day one. We believe real growth happens when you're stretched, supported, and surrounded by smart, passionate teammates who want to win together.
   

  

 Even though we’re spread across countries, we come together often in Sweden for team meetings, social events, and offsites - blending global reach with real human connection.
   

  

 We hire for talent, potential, and attitude - valuing different backgrounds and fresh perspectives. Great ideas come from everywhere, and we’re building a team that reflects the world we want to change.
   

  

*Tandem handles sensitive patient data and will conduct a background check before hiring any candidate.*

title: BMS Engineer Data Centers (Gävle, Sweden)
description:
As a BMS Engineer based in Gävle, Sweden, you will be responsible for the installation, commissioning, maintenance, and support of Building Management Systems (BMS) within mission-critical data center environments.

Working in high-availability data centers, you will ensure that critical building systems operate safely, efficiently, and reliably while supporting both planned maintenance and reactive service activities.

Key Responsibilities

* Install, commission, operate, maintain, and troubleshoot Building Management Systems (BMS) and field control systems within data centers.
* Carry out planned preventative maintenance (PPM), reactive maintenance, and support for critical infrastructure.
* Support automation and controls projects, including commissioning of new installations and system upgrades.
* Modify, program, and interrogate BMS software, control strategies, graphics, and system databases.
* Monitor and optimize HVAC, cooling, ventilation, and other critical building services to maximize energy efficiency and system performance.
* Diagnose faults quickly and implement effective corrective actions to minimize downtime in mission-critical environments.
* Ensure all maintenance and project work is completed in accordance with health, safety, and industry standards.
* Produce detailed service reports, commissioning documentation, and technical records.
* Develop and maintain excellent relationships with clients, contractors, and on-site operations teams.

Required Skills & Experience

* Previous experience working with Building Management Systems (BMS) in commercial or mission-critical environments.
* Experience operating, maintaining, commissioning, and troubleshooting HVAC and mechanical building services.
* Experience with Niagara BMS systems.
* Experience with BMS software programming, graphics, alarms, trends, and control strategies.
* Excellent fault-finding and problem-solving skills in live operational environments.
* Good understanding of Health & Safety regulations, risk assessments, and safe working practices.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to prepare technical documentation and build positive customer relationships.
* Experience working in data centers or other critical infrastructure environments is highly desirable.

Interviews will be conducted on an ongoing basis, so we encourage interested candidates to apply as soon as possible.

Please send your CV and application to **faktura@ehsexperterna.se**.

We look forward to hearing from you and welcoming the right candidate to our growing team.

title: Head of Engineering
description:
We are looking for an experienced AI native **Head of Engineering** for Redpine. This is a builder's role first and foremost. You will lead, but you will also be deep in the code, shaping the architecture and shipping alongside the team. You will own the how of engineering at Redpine end to end: the team, the systems, and the pace at which we ship. You report directly to our CPTO and co-founder.

**Why this role matters**
-------------------------


Redpine sits in a category that moves every week. The data layer for agents is being defined right now, and the model capabilities underneath it change faster than most teams can absorb. We cannot stand still, and we cannot chase every new result either. We need an engineering leader who can tell the difference, keep a growing team focused, and make sure the right new ideas reach the codebase before our competitors ship them. The job is to make the stay calm while shipping fast

**Responsibilities and Ownership**
----------------------------------

### **The team**

* Build a culture and a team where strong engineers do their best work: clear ownership, low ceremony, high trust.
* Coach engineers, and create the conditions for them to lead.
* Inspire new ways of working and ensure that they get adopted without degrading speed

### **Technical direction**

* Own the architecture across the platform: data pipelines, retrieval and reranking, the knowledge graph, and the API, MCP, and CLI that agents use.
* Recommend the build, buy, and bet calls together to CPTO, guided by the product strategy, and keep the system simple as it grows.
* Stay accountable for reliability, latency, and cost, the things customers actually feel.

### **Delivery and pace**

* Set how the team plans, prioritizes, and ships, and keep cycle time short.
* Remove blockers before they spread, and decide quickly when a decision is reversible.
* Hold a high quality bar without turning the process into a tax.
* Always ask how the team can be faster but still deliver quality software.

### **Staying at the frontier**

* Track the research and the model releases that matter to Redpine, and read them closely enough to hold a real view.
* Separate the results that change how we should build from the ones that are noise.
* Bring the useful ones back to the team in a way that makes people faster, not anxious. Adoption before admiration.

**What we are looking for**
---------------------------

* You have led engineering teams and been accountable for what they shipped. This is a hard requirement, not a nice to have.
* You have done this at a startup that scaled, ideally one we would recognize, and you can speak to what broke as it grew and what you did about it.
* You stay close to the frontier by choice. You read the AI model cards and the papers, and you can say which new capability changes our plan and which does not.
* You are still technical enough to earn the team's trust. You can write and review code, and be useful at it.
* You make hard ideas land. You can take a dense result and explain, to an engineer and to a non engineer, why it matters and what we do about it.
* You favor small, clear systems, and you question whether a thing needs to be built before you build it.

**Bonus**
---------

* Built in a regulated or data heavy domain (health, science, legal, or finance).
* Hands on experience with retrieval, RAG, knowledge graphs, agents, or large scale data infrastructure.
* Took a team through a real scaling step, from seed to whatever came next.
* A public footprint you can point to: open source, writing, talks, or research.

### **About Redpine**


Redpine is an AI-native, global-first company building the knowledge layer for AI. We unlock compliant access to high-quality, non-public, multi-modal data in fields like medicine, science, law, and finance, enabling data owners to monetize their content in a controlled way.


We embrace a culture of curiosity, speed, innovation and excellence. We believe in continuous learning, experimentation, and pushing the boundaries of what's possible in AI technology. You'll be working closely with the founding team, including Anders (ex-partner at VC, McKinsey), and David (ex-tech and product leader at unicorn companies Spotify, Zettle and Lunar).


We prioritize an office-first culture (in Stockholm) to fuel collaboration and connection, but we’re big on autonomy - for those days you need a "deep work" day at home or have life admin to handle.


If you are eager to make a meaningful impact in the AI space, we’d love to hear from you. We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive team: if you're excited about this role but your experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway.
